---
title: "RLMHG08: Historical BAS number-skills growth, waves 1-4 (Byrne et al.)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

RLMHG08 is a per-measure sibling of `lrp-rlm-hg-001` (word reading): a descriptive
group-by-wave growth model for BAS number skills over the four
annual waves
of the Byrne, MacDonald & Buckley reading-language-memory cohort
(`study_id="rlm"`), for the three reading groups (Down syndrome, average readers,
reading-matched), on a waves-1-3 complete-case core that
reproduces the paper's Table 2, extended by the later waves where children in
that core were observed.

::: {.callout-important title="Descriptive natural-history, NOT an intervention effect"}

`readgrp` is a **cohort factor**, not a randomised or assigned condition, and this
model carries no treatment, baseline-as-precision, or adjustment-set structure.
Every quantity here is a **descriptive** group-by-wave expected score or growth
rate — natural-history evidence, not a causal effect. The intervention-effect
estimands live in the RLI (`lrp-rli-...`) suite.

:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Provisional Beta-Binomial ceiling"}

The Beta-Binomial denominator ($N = 60$) is the **observed maximum** in the
prepared extract, not a manual-confirmed instrument ceiling
(`n_trials_confirmed=False`). Confirming the true maximum against the instrument
manual is a data-owner decision
(`notes/202607021052-issue-164-byrne-followup-plan.md`, decision 3); until then,
read the fitted cells as provisional. The 1983 Basic Number Skills item count is unverified; the observed
maximum of 60 rules out the 46-item BAS-II scale, so the true ceiling is at
least 60. The shared prior scales are reused from
`lrp-rlm-hg-001`; the prior-predictive check below flags any miscalibration.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Audit baseline"}

The model is fit on the per-measure complete-case subset (subjects with this
measure observed at
all three core waves). The observed group-by-wave means reproduce
the paper's Table 2 complete-case figures for this measure and are written beside
the posterior summaries as the audit baseline.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Extended follow-up window (#338)"}

Wave 4 is an **extension wave** outside the complete-case rule: children
in the core subset contribute wherever this measure was observed. The
wave-4 cells are therefore an attrition-selected follow-up tail with their
own per-cell `n` (reported in the tables). BAS number skills was not
assessed at wave 5, so the window ends at the common waves-1-4 horizon.

:::

## Model

For child $i$ in reading group $g[i]$ at wave $t$, the bounded BAS number skills
count (denominator $N = 60$) is Beta-Binomial with an age-free, group-by-wave
population mean and a stable per-child offset:

$$
\text{score}_{it} \sim \mathrm{BetaBinomial}(N,\ p_{it},\ \kappa_{g[i]}),
\qquad
\operatorname{logit}(p_{it}) = \eta_{g[i],\,t} + u_i .
$$

$\eta_{g,t}$ is the population expected score for each **supported**
group-by-wave cell (waves 1-4 in all three groups; this measure was not assessed at wave 5 —
no prior-only cells); $u_i$ is a non-centred, **group-centred** per-child
random intercept ($u_i = (z_i - \bar z_{g[i]})\,\sigma_{\text{subject},\,g[i]}$)
so the group-by-wave level $\eta$ carries the group mean. Both the
between-child SD $\sigma_{\text{subject},\,g}$ and the measurement
overdispersion $\kappa_g$ are **indexed by group** (#338, follow-up-plan
decision 7). Deterministic quantities report the expected item score per
cell and within-group growth over the common waves-1-4 window; the
extension intervals and the pairwise total-growth (wave 1 to wave 4)
contrasts are summarised from the fitted cells on the children observed at
both endpoint waves.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_historical_growth.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
